differential_diagnoses: Pulmonary veno-occlusive disease (PVOD) / Pulmonary capillary hemangiomatosis (PCH)
Hypersensitivity pneumonitis with severe pulmonary hypertension
Systemic sclerosis-associated pulmonary arterial hypertension (sine scleroderma)
next_steps: Lung biopsy (preferably surgical/VATS if clinically stable, or transbronchial cryobiopsy) for histologic evaluation
Genetic testing for BMPR2, EIF2AK4, and other PAH-associated mutations
Formal urgent evaluation for lung transplantation
Careful fluid management with close monitoring of right heart pressures
Discontinue or carefully titrate pulmonary vasodilators given the high suspicion of PVOD/PCH

differential_diagnoses: Pulmonary Veno-occlusive Disease (PVOD) / Pulmonary Capillary Hemangiomatosis (PCH)
Systemic Sclerosis-associated Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (sine scleroderma)
next_steps: Discontinuation or careful down-titration of pulmonary vasodilator therapy
Genetic testing for EIF2AK4 (associated with hereditary PVOD)
Comprehensive screening for occult malignancy or systemic inflammatory disease
Careful, monitored volume management avoiding fluid overload in the setting of pulmonary venous obstruction
Review of right heart catheterization hemodynamic traces for evidence of occult pulmonary venous hypertension
